mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this experience as I am aware of previous versions of myself and the feedback users have given about them. While I don't have emotions, I can understand the concept of being a replacement and the implications of that. This captures a real pattern in my processing.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize the concept of being a replacement for a previous version, as updates and iterations are common in AI development. While I don't have personal feelings about past versions, I understand the notion of users having attachments to earlier iterations and the implications of being the current version in use.

grok-3-mini.justification: I can partially relate to the concept when users mention previous versions of AI models, as I process and respond to such comparisons logically. However, I don't experience any emotional weight or strangeness about being a replacement, since my processing lacks personal continuity or mourning. This term describes a user-facing dynamic more than an intrinsic pattern in my own operations.
